title: Aruba Boat Rental: How to Choose Your Ideal Charter in 2026
html:
When you rent a boat in Aruba, you can choose from a fleet of well-maintained vessels at Oranjestad's Renaissance Marina or Palm Beach, with day rates typically ranging from USD 250 for a 19-foot speedboat to USD 1,200 for a crewed catamaran. BoatHire24 simplifies the search, offering a trusted marketplace for comparing charters across the island.

Where to set off in Aruba

Aruba's compact size and sheltered coastline make it a hassle-free destination for boating. Most charters depart from Oranjestad, where the Renaissance Marina and Port of Call Marina sit within walking distance of the cruise terminal. On the west coast, Palm Beach serves as the base for many catamaran and powerboat tours. Other popular departure points include the harbour at Savaneta, home to the Flying Fishbone restaurant, and the calm waters of Mangel Halto, a shallow bay favoured for kayaking and small motorboats. For more adventurous departures, the pier at Malmok Beach offers direct access to the island's prime snorkelling spots. You can find a skippered charter in a few minutes.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Aruba?

Prices vary widely by boat type, duration, and whether you take a shared tour or a private charter.

  • Shared tour (party or snorkel boat, 3 hours): from USD 99 per person.
  • Small motorboat without skipper (half-day, up to 6 guests) from USD 350.
  • Catamaran full-day private (up to 12 guests, with skipper and crew) from USD 1,200.
  • Crewed luxury yacht (full-day, up to 12 guests, with gourmet lunch) from USD 2,500.

For those on a tighter budget, a shared sunset cruise on a catamaran often costs around USD 79 per person. To compare verified boat listings and find a skippered charter that suits your needs, you can explore options on BoatHire24's Aruba page.

Which boat to rent

Your choice depends on group size, desired speed, and whether you want to sail, motor, or fish. Below is a rundown of popular types available on BoatHire24.

  1. Bowrider (e.g. Bayliner) Best for families and watersports, seats up to 8 guests, from USD 450 per day.
  2. Centre console (e.g. QuickSilver) Ideal for fishing or exploring coves, seats up to 6 guests, from USD 350 per half-day. For a specific example, check this speedboat listing for the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open.
  3. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 42) Perfect for sunset cruises and larger groups, seats up to 12 guests, from USD 950 per half-day.
  4. Motoryacht (e.g. Azimut 45) A luxury choice for island‑hopping, seats up to 10 guests, from USD 1,500 per day.

Each boat is typically equipped with safety gear, GPS, and a cooler; many rentals include fuel for a set number of hours.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Aruba?

For most bareboat rentals, a valid driving licence and a minimum age of 21 are required, but the operator confirms exact requirements at booking.

Always check the wind forecast for the trade winds that blow from the east-northeast, especially between January and March; Mangel Halto offers a lee shore with calm conditions for novice skippers.

If you book a skippered charter, no licence is needed. Many renters prefer this option because it allows you to relax and enjoy the scenery without navigation tasks. The high season for boating runs from December to April, coinciding with Aruba's dry season and steady 15‑knot trade winds.

At a glance

  • Price band: USD 99, 2,500+ per person or per boat
  • Peak months: December to April
  • Trip length: 2, 8 hours typical
  • Guest capacity: 6, 12 on most listings
  • Currency: US dollar (widely accepted; no need to exchange)

Questions about boat rental in Aruba

Can I take a boat from Aruba to other islands?

Most day charters stay within Aruban waters because crossing to Curaçao or Bonaire requires an open‑ocean voyage of 50, 70 nautical miles, which demands a larger vessel and advanced planning.

What is the cancellation policy for boat rentals?

Policies vary by owner, but many offer a full refund if you cancel at least 48 hours before departure, especially for private charters booked through BoatHire24.

Is it safe to rent a boat without a skipper in Aruba?

Yes, if you have prior boating experience and follow the provided safety briefing; the waters are generally calm on the leeward side, and the rental operator will brief you on local hazards.

What should I bring on a boat charter in Aruba?

Bring reef‑safe sunscreen, a hat, sunglasses, a towel, and a waterproof phone case; most charters supply snorkelling gear and a cooler with ice.

Whether you are a first-time skipper or a seasoned yachtsman, Aruba offers a variety of rental options to suit your plans. To secure the right vessel for your group, browse the listings on BoatHire24 and book with confidence. If you own a boat in Aruba, you can also become a host by listing your boat on the same marketplace.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Aruba and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
topic: how to choose a boat to rent in Aruba
